sourceDesc.p: Derived from CDSL MW, PWG, PWK, AP90, GRA, and FRI source records for interoperability analysis.

projectDesc.p: tei-archival-profile-v0.1; TEI is used as the archival/textual model. Raw CDSL records are preserved as escaped source-entry quotes, while selected lexical structures are promoted into TEI entry, form, sense, citation, and relation elements.

revisionDesc.change: Included in the full machine-reviewed TEI profile.

sense.note: No stable machine definition could be extracted; source record is preserved in cit/quote.

listBibl.head: Machine-extracted citation index; source quotes preserve the archival text.

cit.quote: <L>11185.1<pc>61,1<k1>aBi<k2>aBi/<e>1A ¬¶ (As a separate adverb or preposition) it expresses (with <ab>acc.</ab>) to, towards, in the direction of, against<info lex="inh"/> <LEND>

<L>4471<pc>1-0328<k1>aBi<k2>aBi/
{#aBi/#}¬¶ zend. {%aiwi%},
<lang n="greek">·ºÄŒºœÜŒØ</lang>, lat. {%ob%}, ahd. {%umbi%}, nhd. {%um%},
<lang n="Old-Church-Slavonic">–æ–±—ä</lang>. Die begriffliche Verwandtschaft tritt bei {#aBitas#} deutlich hervor. <is>Upasarga</is> (
<ls>NIR. 1,3.</ls>), <is>Gati</is> und <is>Karmapravacanƒ´ya</is>
<ls>P. 1,4,56.</ls> <ls n="P. 1,4,">59.</ls> <ls n="P. 1,4,">60.</ls> <ls n="P. 1,4,">91.</ls> <is>ga·πáa</is> {#prAdi#};
<ls>VOP. 1,8.</ls>
<div n="1"> 1) <lex>adv.</lex>
<div n="2"> a) {%herbei%}: {#raTa^M devAso a\Bi vi\kzu vA^ja\yum#}
<ls>·πöV. 2,31,2.</ls> <ab>Gegens.</ab> {#apa#} (<ab>s. d.</ab>). Sehr h√§ufig in Verbindung mit Wurzeln der {%Bewegung.%}
<div n="2">‚Äî b) {%hinein%}: {#aByaBi#} (in die Becher) {#somAnunnayanti#}
<ls>KƒÄTY. ≈öR. 22,10,5.</ls>
<div n="2">‚Äî c) {%oben, in der H√∂he%} (?): {#DanAdDarmaH praBavati SElAdaBi nadI yaTA#}
<ls>MBH. 12,228.</ls> <ab>Vgl.</ab> {#aBikrama#} .
<div n="2">‚Äî d) in Zusammensetzungen mit einem nomen dr√ºckt {#aBi#} eine {%Steigerung%} aus; <ab>vgl.</ab> {#aBiDarma, aBitAmra, aBinamra, aBinava#} .
<div n="1">‚Äî 2) <ab>praep.</ab> mit <ab>folg.</ab> oder <ab>vorang.</ab> <ab>acc.</ab>
<div n="2"> a) {%zu ‚Äî her, zu ‚Äî hin, nach ‚Äî hin, gegen%}: {#pra taM na^ya prata\raM vasyo\ acCA\Bi su\mnaM de\vaBa^ktam#}
<ls>·πöV. 10,45,9.</ls> {#sa mAnu^zIra\Bi viSo\ vi BA^ti#}
<ls n="·πöV.">7,5,2.</ls> {#vAjA^~ a\Bi pa^vamAna\ pra gA^hase#}
<ls n="·πöV.">9,110,2.</ls> <ls n="·πöV.">1,31,18.</ls> <ls n="·πöV. 1,">45,8.</ls> <ls n="·πöV. 1,">48,7.</ls> <ls n="·πöV. 1,">119,1.</ls> <ls n="·πöV.">8,82,4.</ls> <ls n="·πöV.">10,18,8.</ls> <ls>SV. II,9,3,6,2.</ls> <ls>≈öAT. BR. 1,2,3,4.</ls> <ls n="≈öAT. BR.">4,1,5,16.</ls> <ls n="≈öAT. BR.">6,6,1,12.</ls> <ls n="≈öAT. BR.">11,5,5,6.</ls> {#puruzamaByAvfttam#} {%nach dem%} <ab>p.</ab> {%hingekehrt%}
<ls n="≈öAT. BR.">7,5,1,7.</ls> {#agnimaBi SalaBAH patanti#}
<ls>P. 2,1,14</ls>, <ab>Sch.</ab>
{#vfkzamaBi dyotate#}
<ls n="P.">1,4,91</ls>, <ab>Sch.</ab> Verbindet sich mit dem <ab>obj.</ab> auch zu einem <lex>adv.</lex> <ab>comp.</ab>: {#Saram ‚Äî SabdaM prati gajaprepsuraBilakzyamapAtayam#}
<ls>DA≈ö. 1,22.</ls> {#aByagni SalaBAH patanti#}
<ls>P. 2,1,14</ls>, <ab>Sch.</ab>
{#aByarkabimbaM sTitaH#}
<ls>≈öƒÄK. 170.</ls> Dies ist das {#aBi ABimuKye#} (
<ls>NIR. 1,3.</ls>
<ls>P. 2,1,14.</ls>
<ls>H. an. 7,36.</ls>
<ls>MED. avy. 49.</ls>) und {#lakzaRe#} (
<ls>P. 1,4,91.</ls> <ls n="P.">2,1,14.</ls>
<ls>H. an.</ls>) oder {#cihne#}
(<ls>VOP. 5,7.</ls> mit dem Beispiele: {#prAjYo govindamaBi tizWati#} {%ein Weiser h√§lt sich zu%} <is>Gov</is>.).
<div n="2">‚Äî b) {%in ‚Äî hinein%}: {#aBi SuklAni ca kfzRAni ca lomAni nizyUto#} ({%eingeflochten%}) {#Bavati (rukmaH)#}
<ls>≈öAT. BR. 11,5,5,6</ls>; <ab>vgl.</ab> das zweite Beispiel <ab>u.</ab> {%h.%}
<div n="2">‚Äî c) {%um, f√ºr%}
[Page1-0329]
(<ab>d. h.</ab> {%zur Gewinnung von%}): {#e\te a^sfgra\minda^vasti\raH pa\vitra^mA\Sava^H . viSvA^nya\Bi sOBa^gA#}
<ls n="·πöV.">9,62,1.</ls> {#yenA^ na\H pUrve^ pi\tara^H pada\jYAH sva\rvido^ a\Bi gA adri^mu\zRan#}
<ls n="·πöV. 9,">97,39. 1,33,11. 61,10.</ls> {#ya\maBi#} (so ist zu lesen) {#kA\mamu\ttarARi havI\Mzi nirva\pati#}
<ls>≈öAT. BR. 2,2,1,5.</ls>
<div n="2">‚Äî d) {%aus Anlass von, wegen%}: {#a\nyasya^ ci\ttama\Bi sa^Mca\reRyamu\tADI^ta\M vi na^Syati#}
<ls>·πöV. 1,170,1.</ls> {#pava^mAnA asfkzata\ somA^H Su\krAsa\ inda^vaH . a\Bi viSvA^ni\ kAvyA^ ..#}
<ls n="·πöV.">9,63,25.</ls>
<div n="2">‚Äî e) {%gegen, in Bezug auf, auf, √ºber%}: {#etadDa sma sa tadaByAha#} (so ist zu lesen)
<ls>≈öAT. BR. 1,1,2,11.</ls> {#kzatriyamaByavivAdinIm#}
<ls n="≈öAT. BR.">3,9,3,3.</ls> {#tasya tvayamAtmA yAvadidamaByayamagnirvihitaH#}
<ls n="≈öAT. BR.">8,1,4,1.</ls> <ls n="≈öAT. BR.">7,2,4,28.</ls> <ls n="≈öAT. BR.">11,5,5,12.</ls> {#AttamaBi mimIte#}
<ls>KƒÄTY. ≈öR. 10,1,5.</ls> {#SAntasaMkalpaH sumanA yaTA syAdvItamanyurgOtamo mABi#}
<ls>KA·π¨HOP. 1,10.</ls> {#sADurdevadatto mAtaramaBi#}
<ls>P. 1,4,91</ls>, <ab>Sch.</ab>
{#na tAmaBi krudDo#}
(<ls>BOLL.</ls> verbindet) {#muniH#}
<ls>VIKR. 36,1.</ls> {#anyo'nyamaBi saMrabDO#} (<ab>vgl.</ab> {#tvAmeva prati saMrabDaH#}
<ls>R. 6,4,30.</ls>
<ls>GORR.</ls> verbindet an beiden Orten die <ab>praep.</ab> mit dem <ab>partic.</ab>; √ºbrigens kommt
<ls n="GORR.">6,3,17.</ls> auch ein {#aBisaMrabDa#} ohne <ab>obj.</ab> vor, und {#anyo'nyam#} kann f√ºglich auch als <lex>adv.</lex> gefasst werden)
<ls>R. 6,76,32.</ls> Dies ist das {#aBi itTaMBUtAKyAne#}
<ls>P. 1,4,91.</ls> {#itTaMBUtakaTane#}
<ls>MED. avy. 49.</ls> {#itTaMBUte#}
<ls>H. an. 7,36.</ls> {#itTaMBAve#}
<ls>VOP. 5,7.</ls> (mit dem Beispiele: {#Bakto-viBumaBi#} {%dem%} <is>Vi·π£·πáu</is> {%zugethan, sein Anh√§nger%}).
<div n="2">‚Äî f) {%bei%} (mit <ab>verbb.</ab> des {%Bittens.%} zur Bezeichnung dessen, bei welchem gebeten wird): {#a\Bi tvA^ deva savitarBA\gamI^mahe#}
<ls>·πöV. 1,24,3.</ls> {#a\Bi de\vA~ iya^kzate#}
<ls n="·πöV.">9,11,1.</ls>
<div n="2">‚Äî g) {%√ºber%}: {#dyOrna BUmA\Bi#}
<ls>·πöV. 6,36,5.</ls> {#sA\hvAnviSvA^ a\Bi spfDa^H#}
<ls n="·πöV.">9,20,1.</ls> {#kenA\Bi ma\hnA parva^tA\nkena\ karmA^Ri\ pUru^zaH#}
<ls>AV. 10,2,18.</ls> Verbindet sich mit dem <ab>reg.</ab> Worte zu einem <ab>comp.</ab>, <ab>s.</ab> {#aBivAsas#} . <ab>Vgl.</ab> {#as#} und {#BU#} mit {#aBi#} .
<div n="2">‚Äî h) in <ab>distrib.</ab> <ab>Bed.</ab> ({#vIpsAyAm#})
<ls>P. 1,4,91.</ls>
<ls>VOP. 5,7.</ls>
<ls>H. an. 7,36.</ls> {#vfkzaM vfkzamaBi siYcati#} {%er begiesst einen Baum nach dem andern%}
<ls>P. 1,4,91</ls>, <ab>Sch.</ab>
{#BUtaM BUtamaBi praBuH#} <is>Vi·π£·πáu</is> {%ist in jedem Wesen%}
<ls>VOP. 5,7.</ls> - <ls>MED. avy. 49.</ls> wird noch ein {#aBi aBilAze#} erw√§hnt, wobei an {#aBilAza#} selbst, an {#aBikANkzA#} <ab>u. s. w.</ab> gedacht worden ist. ‚Äî {#aBi#} schliesst sich theils allein, theils mit andern <ab>praepp.</ab> an eine grosse Anzahl von Verbalwurzeln <ab>an.</ab> Nicht selten entspricht {#aBi#} der deutschen Partikel {%be%}: {#varz#} {%regnen%}, {#aBivarz#} {%beregnen%}, <ab>u. s. w.</ab>
<LEND>

<L>7252<pc>1-084-c<k1>aBi<k2>aBi/ {#aBi/#}¬¶ <div n="1">‚Äî 1„Äâ <lex>Adv.</lex> <div n="2">‚Äî a„Äâ {%herbei%}. <div n="2">‚Äî b„Äâ {%dar√ºber hinaus%}. <div n="1">‚Äî 2„Äâ <ab>Praep.</ab> <div n="2">‚Äî a„Äâ mit <ab>Acc.</ab> <div n="3">‚Äî Œ±„Äâ {%zu ‚Äî her, zu ‚Äî hin, nach ‚Äî hin, gegen%}. <div n="3">‚Äî Œ≤„Äâ {%in ‚Äî hinein%} <ls>≈öAT. BR. 6,7,1,7</ls>. <div n="3">‚Äî Œ≥„Äâ {%um, f√ºr, zur Gewinnung von%}. <div n="3">‚Äî Œ¥„Äâ {%√ºber ‚Äî hinaus%}. [Page1-085-a] <div n="3">‚Äî Œµ„Äâ {%um ‚Äî willen%}. <div n="3">‚Äî Œ∂„Äâ {%gegen, in Bezug auf, auf, √ºber%}. <div n="3">‚Äî Œ∑„Äâ *in distributiver <ab>Bed.</ab>, die aber schon im wiederholten <ab>Acc.</ab> liegt. <div n="2">‚Äî b„Äâ mit <ab>Abl.</ab> <div n="3">‚Äî Œ±„Äâ {%mit Ausschluss von, ohne%} <ls>·πöV. 1,139,8. 10,25,3</ls>. <div n="3">‚Äî Œ≤„Äâ {%hervor, ‚Äî aus%} <ls>Spr. 3573</ls> (wohl fehlerhaft f√ºr {#aDi#}). <LEND>

<L>2991<pc>0126-c<k1>aBi<k2>aBi {#aBi#}¬¶ {%<ab>ind.</ab>%} {@1@} (As a prefix to verbs and nouns) It means ({%a%}) ‚Äòto‚Äô, ‚Äòtowards‚Äô, ‚Äòin the direction of‚Äô; {#aBigam#} go towards, {#aBiyA, ÀögamanaM#}, {#ÀöyAnaM#} &c.; ({%b%}) ‚Äòfor‚Äô, ‚Äòagainst‚Äô; {#Àölaz, Àöpat#} &c.; ({%c%}) ‚Äòon‚Äô, ‚Äòupon‚Äô; {#ÀösiMc#} to sprinkle on &c.; ({%d%}), ‚Äòover‚Äô, ‚Äòabove‚Äô, ‚Äòacross‚Äô; {#ÀöBU#} to <lbinfo n="over+power"/> overpower, {#Àötan#}; ({%e%}) ‚Äògreatly‚Äô; <lbinfo n="‚Äòexces+sively"/> ‚Äòexcessively‚Äô {#ÀökaMp#}. {@--2@} (As a prefix to nouns not derived from verbs, and to <lbinfo n="ad+jectives"/> adjectives) It expresses ({%a%}) <lbinfo n="inten+sity"/> intensity or superiority; {#ÀöDarmaH#} ‚Äòsupreme duty‚Äô; {#ÀötAmra#} ‚Äòvery red, {#Àönava#} ‚Äòvery new‚Äô; ({%b%}) ‚Äòtowards,‚Äô ‚Äòin the <lbinfo n="direc+tion"/> direction of,‚Äô forming <ab>adv.</ab> compounds; {#ÀöcEdyaM, ÀömuKaM, ÀödUti#} &c. {@--3@} (As a separable adverb) It means <lbinfo n="to+wards"/> towards, in the direction or <lbinfo n="vicini+ty"/> vicinity of (<ab>opp.</ab> {#apa#}); in, above, aloft, on the top, (mostly Ved). {@--4@} (As a preposition with <ab>acc.</ab>) ({%a%}) To, <lbinfo n="to+wards"/> towards, in the direction of, against; (with <ab>acc.</ab> or in <ab>comp.</ab> in this sense); {#aByAgni#} or {#agnimaBi SalaBAH#} {#pataMti; vfkzamaBi dyotate vidyut#} <ls>Sk.</ls>; <lbinfo n="ls:≈öi.+ 9. 56"/> <ls>≈öi. 9. 56</ls>, <ls n="≈öi.">7. 40</ls>; {#aByarkabiMbaM sTitaH#} <ls>≈ö. 7. 11.</ls> ({%b%}) Near, before, in front or presence of; <ls>≈öi. 7. 32</ls>; <ls n="≈öi.">15. 58.</ls> ({%c%}) On, upon, with regard or <lbinfo n="re+ference"/> reference to; {#sAyamaMqanamaBi tvarayaMtyaH#} <lbinfo n="ls:Ki.+ 9. 6"/> <ls>Ki. 9. 6</ls>; {#sADurdevadatto mAtaramaBi#} <ls>Sk.</ls> ({%d%}) Severally, one after another (in a distributive sense); {#vfkzaM vfkzamaBi#} <lbinfo n="siM#cati"/> {#siMcati#} <ls>Sk.</ls>; {#BUtaBUtamaBi praBuH#} <ls>Bop.</ls> By <lbinfo n="ls:P.+I"/> [Page0127-a+ 57] <ls>P. I. 4. 91</ls> {#aBi#} has all the senses of {#anu#} given in <ls n="P.">I. 4. 90</ls> except that of {#BAga#}; {%<ab>e. g.</ab>%} ({#lakzaRe#}) {#harimaBi vartate#}; ( <lbinfo n="itTaM#BUtAKyAne"/> {#itTaMBUtAKyAne#}) {#Bakto harimaBi#}; ({#vIpsAyAM#}), {#devaM#} {#devamaBi siMcati#}; but {#yadatra mamABizyAt taddIyatAM#}; {#prAjYo goviMdamaBitizWati#} <ls>Bop.</ls> ({%e%}) In, into, to; <ls>≈öi. 8. 60.</ls> ({%f%}) For, for the sake of, on account of (<ab>Ved.</ab>). According to <ls>G. M.</ls> {#aBi#} has these senses: <lbinfo n="--a#Bi"/> {#--aBi#}, <lbinfo n="pUjABfSArTecCAsOmyABimuKyasOrUpyavacanAhA#rasvADyAyezu"/> {#pUjABfSArTecCAsOmyABimuKyasOrUpyavacanAhArasvADyAyezu#}; {%<ab>e. g.</ab>%} {#pUjAyAM, aBivaMdate; BfSe#}, {#aBiniveSaH; icCAyAM, aBilAzaH, aBikaH#}; {#sOmye#} or {#mADurye, aBijAtaH; ABimuKye#}, <lbinfo n="aBi#muKaM"/> {#aBimuKaM, aByagni; sOrUpye, aBirUpaM; vacane#}, <lbinfo n="a#BiDatte"/> {#aBiDatte; AhAre, aByavaharati; svADyAye#}, <lbinfo n="aBya#syAti"/> {#aByasyAti#}. [<ab>cf.</ab> <ab>L.</ab> {%ob%}; <ab>Gr.</ab> {%amphi%}; Zend {%aibi%} or {%aiwi%}, <ab>Goth.</ab> {%bi%}; also {%umbi%}; {%um%}]. <LEND>

<L>765<pc>0082<k1>aBi<k2>aBi/ {@abh√≠,@} (abhƒ´ÃÅ),¬¶ in seiner Bedeutung m√∂glichst genau dem <lang>lat.</lang> ob (von dem das oskische op ganz zu trennen ist) entsprechend. Die Grundbedeutung ist zu jemand hin, Gesicht gegen Gesicht gekehrt [ƒÅbhimukhyam <ls>Nir. „Äî1,3„Äï</ls>], also auf die Vorderseite eines Gegenstandes zu, daher weiter: auf einen Gegenstand zu, um ihn zu erlangen, zu benutzen. Endlich dr√ºckt es eine noch √ºber den Gegenstand hinausgehende Bewegung aus: dar√ºber hinaus, √ºberragend, √ºbertreffend. So erscheint es als: <div n="P">Erstes Glied einer Zusammensetzung in den n√§chstfolgenden Worten. <div n="P">Richtungswort, gef√ºgt zu den Verben: aj, a√±j, am, ar, ar·π£, av, <hom>1.</hom> as, i, idh, iyak·π£, ƒ´k·π£, ud, <hom>2.</hom> ≈´h, ·πõ√±j, kƒÅ≈õ, krand, kram, k·π£ad, k·π£am, k·π£ar, ksip, khyƒÅ, gam, <hom>1.</hom> <hom>2.</hom> gƒÅ, gƒÅh, gur, <hom>1.</hom> gir, ghrƒÅ, cak·π£, car, jan, ju·π£, ta·πÉs, <hom>1.</hom> tan, tar, t·πõd, ts√°r, dabh, dah, dƒÅs, <hom>2.</hom> dƒ´, drƒÅ, dru, druh, dhanv, dham, <hom>1.</hom> dhƒÅ, dhƒÅv, dhƒ´, nak·π£, <hom>2.</hom> na≈õ, nƒ´, <hom>1.</hom> <hom>2.</hom> nu, pad, <hom>1.</hom> pƒÅ, pi, pi≈õ, p≈´, prath, pru, pru·π£, bƒÅdh, bha√±j, bhƒÅ, bh≈´, bh·πõ, mad, <hom>1.</hom> man, mantray, mand, (mur), m·πõdh, m·πõ≈õ, mlƒÅ, yaj, (yam), yas, yƒÅ, yuj, yudh, rak·π£, (rabh), ruh, lƒ´, vaj, van, valg, va≈õ, <hom>2.</hom> vas, vah, <hom>1.</hom> vƒÅ, vƒÅ≈õ, vij, vivƒÅs, vƒ´, <hom>1.</hom> v·πõ, v·πõt, v·πõdh, v·πõ·π£, vyƒÅ, vraj, vlag, ≈õa·πÉs, ≈õƒÅs, ≈õuc, ≈õubh, ≈õnath, <hom>1.</hom> ≈õru, ≈õvas, sac, sad, san, sap, sah, s·πõj, stan, stu, sthƒÅ, spa≈õ, sru, svar, han, hary, hv·πõ. <div n="P"><ab>Pr√§p.</ab> <ab n="mit">m.</ab> <ab>Acc.</ab> 1„Äâ {%zu ‚Äî hin%} „Ä∞ mitr√°m {534,10}; „Ä∞ n√°ras {363,7}; „Ä∞ nas {264,11}; {327,3} (durch su getrennt). _{327,4}; „Ä∞ vi≈õas {521,2}; „Ä∞ mƒÅÃÅnu·π£ƒÅn {48,7}; „Ä∞ vats√°m {725,7}; ≈õakt√≠m „Ä∞ {230,7}; „Ä∞ v√°syas {31,18}; „Ä∞ sumn√°m {871,9}; „Ä∞ jƒ´valok√°m {844,8}; „Ä∞ pr√°yas {45,8}; {119,1}; {134,1}; {626,42}; {652,29}; {683,14}; {702,24}; „Ä∞ pr√°yƒÅ·πÉsi {457,44}; {819,25}; „Ä∞ ≈õ≈´ÃÅlam {162,11}; „Ä∞ dhƒÅÃÅma {121,6}; sadh√°stham „Ä∞ {733,3}; „Ä∞ priy√°tame sadh√°sthe {843,6}; „Ä∞ k·π£ƒÅÃÅm {459,13}; {534,16}; „Ä∞ dr√≥·πáƒÅni {715,1}; {745,2}; dr√∫·πáƒÅ {810,2}; sƒÅ≈õanƒÅna≈õan√© „Ä∞ {916,4}; „Ä∞ ≈õukrƒÅÃÅm upast√≠ram {774,28}. 2„Äâ {%zu = um zu erlangen%} „Ä∞ ≈õr√°vas {61,10}; kr√°tum „Ä∞ {450,5}; „Ä∞ sa√∫bhagƒÅ {774,1}; „Ä∞ v√≠≈õvƒÅni kƒÅÃÅviƒÅ {775,25}; gƒÅÃÅs „Ä∞ {784,3}; „Ä∞ gƒÅÃÅs {809,39}; „Ä∞ dy≈´ÃÅn {33,11}; {190,4}. 3„Äâ {%√ºber ‚Äî hinaus,%} √ºberragend, √ºbertreffend „Ä∞ dyƒÅÃÅm {945,8}; bh≈´ÃÅma „Ä∞ {477,5}; car·π£a·πáƒ´ÃÅs „Ä∞ {86,5}; {303,4}; {377,1}; {440,2}; {531,2}; k·π£itƒ´ÃÅs {248,4}; p√°√±ca bh≈´ÃÅma {585,2}; v√≠≈õvƒÅ jƒÅtƒÅÃÅ {697,4}; bh√∫vanƒÅ „Ä∞ {250,4}; {822,9}; p√°tnƒ´s „Ä∞ {398,5}; pa·πáƒ´ÃÅn „Ä∞ {675,10}; „Ä∞ ≈õr√≠yas {278,2}. 4„Äâ {%um ‚Äî willen:%} vratƒÅÃÅ {892,9}. <div n="P">In {798,36} ist zu abh√≠ zu erg√§nzen ar·π£anti aus dem abh√≠ ar·π£asi des vorhergehenden Verses; in {33,9} abhavas oder √§hnliches. <LEND>

<L>481<pc>028<k1>aBi<k2>aBi +prep. + acc. <div n="1"/>1 <lang n="czech">k, proti, do, v, na, nad, kv≈Øli</lang> <div n="1"/>2 <lang n="russian">–∫, –ø—Ä–æ—Ç–∏–≤, –¥–æ, –≤, –Ω–∞, –Ω–∞–¥, —Ä–∞–¥–∏</lang> <div n="1"/>3 <lang n="english">to, towards, against, into, on, upon, over, for, for the sake of</lang> <LEND>
